metadata.title: Food-Effect Bioavailability and Fed Bioequivalence Studies

rules.rule_text: Food-effect BA studies must be conducted for all new chemical entities (NCEs) during the IND period.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that a food-effect BA study be conducted for all new chemical entities (NCEs) during the IND period.

rules.rule_text: Food-effect BA studies must be conducted early in the drug development process to guide and select formulations for further development and to provide information for CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY and/or DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION sections of product labels.
rules.verbatim_quote: Food-effect BA studies should be conducted early in the drug development process to guide and select formulations for further development. Food-effect BA information should be available to design clinical safety and efficacy studies and to provide information for the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY and/or DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION sections of product labels.

rules.rule_text: If a sponsor makes changes in components, composition, and/or method of manufacture in the clinical trial formulation prior to approval, BE must be demonstrated between the to-be-marketed formulation and the clinical trial formulation.
rules.verbatim_quote: If a sponsor makes changes in components, composition, and/or method of manufacture in the clinical trial formulation prior to approval, BE should be demonstrated between the to-be-marketed formulation and the clinical trial formulation.

rules.rule_text: For ANDAs, a BE study under fed conditions must be conducted for all orally administered immediate-release drug products in addition to a BE study under fasting conditions.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to a BE study under fasting conditions, we recommend a BE study under fed conditions for all orally administered immediate-release drug products, with the following exceptions:
rules.exceptions: (1) When both test product and RLD are rapidly dissolving, have similar dissolution profiles, and contain BCS Class I drug substance; (2) When the DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section of the RLD label states the product should be taken only on an empty stomach; (3) When the RLD label does not make any statements about the effect of food on absorption or administration.

rules.rule_text: Food-effect BA and fed BE studies must be performed for all modified-release dosage forms.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend that food-effect BA and fed BE studies be performed for all modified-release dosage forms.

rules.rule_text: For modified-release products in INDs/NDAs, a study comparing BA under fasting and fed conditions must be conducted for all orally administered modified-release drug products.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend a study comparing the BA under fasting and fed conditions for all orally administered modified-release drug products.

rules.rule_text: For ANDAs with modified-release drug products, a BE study under fed conditions must be conducted in addition to a BE study under fasting conditions.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition to a BE study under fasting conditions, a BE study under fed conditions should be conducted for all orally administered modified-release drug products.

rules.rule_text: Food-effect BA and fed BE studies must use a randomized, balanced, single-dose, two-treatment (fed vs. fasting), two-period, two-sequence crossover design.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend a randomized, balanced, single-dose, two-treatment (fed vs. fasting), two-period, two-sequence crossover design for studying the effects of food on the BA of either an immediate-release or a modified-release drug product.
rules.exceptions: A sponsor may propose alternative study designs with scientific rationale and justification provided in the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: For fed BE studies, treatments must consist of both test and reference formulations administered following a test meal (fed condition), and an adequate washout period must separate the two treatments.
rules.verbatim_quote: We recommend a similar, two-treatment, two-period, two-sequence crossover design for a fed BE study except that the treatments should consist of both test and reference formulations administered following a test meal (fed condition). An adequate washout period should separate the two treatments in food-effect BA and fed BE studies.

rules.rule_text: A minimum of 12 subjects must complete food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: A minimum of 12 subjects should complete the food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.exceptions: Studies in patient population are appropriate if safety concerns preclude enrollment of healthy subjects.

rules.rule_text: In general, the highest strength of a drug product intended to be marketed must be tested in food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: In general, the highest strength of a drug product intended to be marketed should be tested in food-effect BA and fed BE studies.
rules.exceptions: Clinical safety concerns can prevent the use of the highest strength and warrant use of lower strengths. For ANDAs, the same lot and strength used in the fasting BE study should be tested in the fed BE study.

rules.rule_text: For products with multiple strengths in ANDAs, if a fed BE study has been performed on the highest strength, BE determination of one or more lower strengths can be waived based on dissolution profile comparisons.
rules.verbatim_quote: For products with multiple strengths in ANDAs, if a fed BE study has been performed on the highest strength, BE determination of one or more lower strengths can be waived based on dissolution profile comparisons (for details see the guidance on Bioavailablity and Bioequivalence Studies for Orally Administered Drug Products - General Considerations).
rules.exceptions: Requires dissolution profile comparisons to support waiver.

rules.rule_text: Food-effect BA and fed BE studies must use a high-fat (approximately 50 percent of total caloric content), high-calorie (approximately 800 to 1000 calories) test meal deriving approximately 150, 250, and 500-600 calories from protein, carbohydrate, and fat respectively.
rules.verbatim_quote: A high-fat (approximately 50 percent of total caloric content of the meal) and high-calorie (approximately 800 to 1000 calories) meal is recommended as a test meal for food-effect BA and fed BE studies. This test meal should derive approximately 150, 250, and 500-600 calories from protein, carbohydrate, and fat, respectively.
rules.exceptions: If caloric breakdown is significantly different, sponsor must provide scientific rationale. In NDAs, exploratory studies may use different meal combinations but one meal must be the high-fat, high-calorie test meal.

rules.rule_text: The caloric breakdown of the test meal must be provided in the study report.
rules.verbatim_quote: The caloric breakdown of the test meal should be provided in the study report.

rules.rule_text: For fasted treatments, subjects must fast for at least 10 hours overnight and must be administered the drug product with 240 mL (8 fluid ounces) of water. No food must be allowed for at least 4 hours post-dose.
rules.verbatim_quote: Following an overnight fast of at least 10 hours, subjects should be administered the drug product with 240 mL (8 fluid ounces) of water. No food should be allowed for at least 4 hours post-dose.
rules.exceptions: Water can be allowed as desired except for one hour before and after drug administration.

rules.rule_text: For fed treatments, subjects must fast overnight for at least 10 hours, start the recommended test meal 30 minutes prior to drug administration, and the drug product must be administered 30 minutes after start of the meal with 240 mL of water.
rules.verbatim_quote: Following an overnight fast of at least 10 hours, subjects should start the recommended meal 30 minutes prior to administration of the drug product. Study subjects should eat this meal in 30 minutes or less; however, the drug product should be administered 30 minutes after start of the meal. The drug product should be administered with 240 mL (8 fluid ounces) of water.
rules.exceptions: No food should be allowed for at least 4 hours post-dose.

rules.rule_text: Timed samples in biological fluid (usually plasma) must be collected to permit characterization of the complete shape of the plasma concentration-time profile for the parent drug.
rules.verbatim_quote: For both fasted and fed treatment periods, timed samples in biological fluid, usually plasma, should be collected from the subjects to permit characterization of the complete shape of the plasma concentration-time profile for the parent drug.
rules.exceptions: It may be advisable to measure other moieties such as active metabolites.

rules.rule_text: The following exposure measures and PK parameters must be obtained from concentration-time curves: AUC(0-inf), AUC(0-t), Cmax, Tmax, lag-time (for modified-release products), terminal elimination half-life, and other relevant PK parameters.
rules.source_section: Section V - Data Analysis and Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: The following exposure measures and pharmacokinetic parameters should be obtained from the resulting concentration-time curves for the test and reference products in food-effect BA and fed BE studies: Total exposure, or area under the concentration-time curve (AUC(0-inf), AUC(0-t)); Peak exposure (Cmax); Time to peak exposure (Tmax); Lag-time (tlag) for modified-release products, if present; Terminal elimination half-life; Other relevant pharmacokinetic parameters

rules.rule_text: Individual subject measurements and summary statistics (group averages, standard deviations, coefficients of variation) must be reported. An equivalence approach using average criterion and log-transformation of AUC and Cmax is required.
rules.source_section: Section V - Data Analysis and Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: Individual subject measurements, as well as summary statistics (e.g., group averages, standard deviations, coefficients of variation) should be reported. An equivalence approach is recommended for food-effect BA (to make a claim of no food effects) and fed BE studies, analyzing data using an average criterion. Log-transformation of exposure measurements (AUC and Cmax) prior to analysis is recommended.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: The 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between test and reference products must be provided for AUC(0-inf), AUC(0-t), and Cmax.
rules.source_section: Section V - Data Analysis and Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: The 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between test and reference products should be provided for AUC(0-inf), AUC(0-t), and Cmax (see guidance for industry on Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence).
rules.exceptions: For IND or NDA food-effect BA studies, the fasted treatment serves as the reference. For ANDA fed BE studies, the RLD administered under fed condition serves as the reference treatment.
cross_references: Statistical Approaches to Establishing Bioequivalence guidance

rules.rule_text: The effect of food on the absorption and BA of a drug product must be described in the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY section of the labeling, and the DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section must provide instructions for drug administration in relation to food.
rules.source_section: Section V - Data Analysis and Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: The effect of food on the absorption and BA of a drug product should be described in the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY section of the labeling. In addition, the DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section of the labeling should provide instructions for drug administration in relation to food based on clinical relevance.

rules.rule_text: For an NDA, absence of food effect on BA is not established if the 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between fed and fasted treatments is not contained in the equivalence limits of 80-125 percent for either AUC(0-inf) (AUC(0-t) when appropriate) or Cmax.
rules.source_section: Section V - Data Analysis and Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: For an NDA, an absence of food effect on BA is not established if the 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between fed and fasted treatments, based on log-transformed data, is not contained in the equivalence limits of 80-125 percent for either AUC(0-inf) (AUC(0-t) when appropriate) or Cmax.
rules.exceptions: Sponsor must provide recommendations on clinical significance of food effect based on total clinical database including dose-response (exposure-response) and/or pharmacokinetic-pharmacodynamic relationships.

rules.rule_text: The results of the food-effect BA study must be reported factually in the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY section of the labeling and form the basis for making label recommendations in the DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section.
rules.source_section: Section V - Data Analysis and Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: The results of the food-effect BA study should be reported factually in the CLINICAL PHARMACOLOGY section of the labeling and should form the basis for making label recommendations (e.g., take only on an empty stomach) in the DOSAGE AND ADMINISTRATION section of the labeling.

rules.rule_text: For an ANDA, BE of a test product to the RLD under fed conditions is concluded when the 90 percent CI for AUC and Cmax is contained in BE limits of 80-125 percent.
rules.source_section: Section V - Data Analysis and Labeling
rules.verbatim_quote: For an ANDA, BE of a test product to the RLD product under fed conditions is concluded when the 90 percent CI for the ratio of population geometric means between the test and RLD product, based on log-transformed data, is contained in the BE limits of 80-125 percent for AUC and Cmax.
rules.exceptions: Although no criterion applies to Tmax, the Tmax values for test and reference products are expected to be comparable based on clinical relevance.

rules.rule_text: When considering dose dumping risk for modified-release dosage forms co-administered with food, consideration must be given to the possibility that food can result in more rapid release of the complete dose than intended.
rules.verbatim_quote: In studying modified-release dosage forms, consideration should be given to the possibility that co-administration with food can result in dose dumping, in which the complete dose may be more rapidly released from the dosage form than intended, creating a potential safety risk for the study subjects.

rules.rule_text: For NDAs involving labeling of drug products (e.g., controlled-release capsules containing beads) to be sprinkled on soft foods, additional in vivo relative BA studies must be performed comparing sprinkled product on soft foods to intact form administration on an empty stomach.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the labeling to indicate that the drug product can be sprinkled on soft foods, additional in vivo relative BA studies should be performed by sprinkling the product on the soft foods to be listed in the labeling (test treatment) and comparing it to the product administered in the intact form (reference treatment), then administering both on an empty stomach.

rules.rule_text: For ANDAs involving sprinkle products, BE of the test to RLD must be demonstrated in a single dose crossover study where both treatments are sprinkled on soft food (usually applesauce) and BE data analyzed using average BE and 90 percent CI criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: In ANDAs, BE of the test to the RLD is demonstrated in a single dose crossover study. Both treatments should be sprinkled on one of the soft foods mentioned in the labeling, usually applesauce. The BE data should be analyzed using average BE and the 90 percent CI criteria should be used to declare BE.

rules.rule_text: For ANDAs involving special vehicle products (oral solutions to be mixed with beverages), BE of the test to RLD must be demonstrated in a single dose crossover study, both treatments mixed with the same listed beverage, using average BE and 90 percent CI criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: In ANDAs, BE of the test to the RLD is demonstrated in a single-dose crossover study. Both treatments should be mixed with one of the beverages mentioned in the labeling. Sponsors should provide evidence that BE differences would not be expected from the use of other listed vehicles. The BE data should be analyzed using average BE, and the 90 percent CI criteria should be used to declare BE.
